about.title: About Services Status
about.description: Helping communities track and share infrastructure and utility service status

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.content: Services Status was created to help communities during infrastructure and utility outages. By providing real-time crowdsourced information about service availability (electricity, water, gas, communications, mobile networks, and road conditions), we help people make informed decisions and support each other during emergencies.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.content: Users can report their location and the status of various services (electricity, water, gas, communications, mobile networks, road blockages), which is displayed on our interactive map. This crowdsourced information helps others understand the extent of outages and find areas where services are available.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.content: We respect your privacy. When you share your location, we only store the coordinates and service status. We don't collect personal information or track individual users. Your contributions help the community while maintaining your anonymity.

getInvolved.title: Get Involved
getInvolved.description: Help us improve Services Status and support your community
getInvolved.content: Services Status is a community-driven project. The more people contribute, the more valuable it becomes for everyone. Here's how you can help:

items.report: Report service status regularly, especially during outages
items.share: Share Services Status with friends, family, and neighbors
items.feedback: Provide feedback to help us improve the platform
items.volunteer: Volunteer your skills if you're a developer, designer, or community organizer

items.plannedMaintenance: Planned maintenance notifications from service providers
items.iotIntegration: Integration with IoT sensors for automated reporting
items.providerNotifications: Direct service provider notifications and two-way communication
items.aiPrediction: AI-powered outage prediction based on historical patterns
items.mobileApp: Mobile app for iOS and Android
items.alerts: SMS/email alerts for service disruptions in your area
items.emergencyServices: Integration with emergency services and first responders
items.detailedReports: Detailed outage reports with estimated restoration times

blog.title: Service Outage Best Practices
blog.subtitle: Insights and tips to keep you safe and prepared during utility and infrastructure service outages.
blog.description: Learn good practices to stay safe and prepared during utility and infrastructure service outages.

lighting.title: Prepare Emergency Lighting
lighting.excerpt: Keep your family safe and see clearly when the lights go out.
content: When a power outage strikes, sudden darkness can cause panic and accidents. Preparation is key. Ensure every accessible room has a flashlight, and keep a stash of fresh batteries in a known location.

While candles create a cozy atmosphere, they pose a significant fire hazard, especially during emergencies when emergency services might be delayed. Opt for battery-operated LED lanterns instead; they provide broader area lighting and are safe to leave on for extended periods.
Consider investing in headlamps for every family member. They allow for hands-free operation, which is crucial when cooking, performing repairs, or attending to children and pets in the dark.

preserveFoodWater.title: Preserve Food and Water
preserveFoodWater.excerpt: Learn how to keep your perishables fresh and stock up on essentials.
content: Your refrigerator and freezer are your first line of defense for food preservation, but they need help. Keep the doors closed as much as possible. An unopened refrigerator will keep food cold for about 4 hours, while a full freezer can hold its temperature for nearly 48 hours.
Stock up on non-perishable food items that require no cooking or refrigeration, such as canned goods, dry fruits, and nuts. Don't forget a manual can opener!

Water is critical. Store at least one gallon of water per person per day for at least three days. If you have advanced warning of a storm, fill your bathtub and spare containers with water for washing and flushing toilets.

stayInformed.title: Stay Informed
stayInformed.excerpt: Use battery-powered radios and charged devices to stay updated.
content: Information is as vital as supplies during a crisis. A battery-powered or hand-crank NOAA weather radio can be a lifesaver when cell towers fail or internet access is down.
Sign up for local emergency alerts and warnings systems in your community. These often provide the most specific and timely instructions for your area.
Keep a list of emergency phone numbers written down on paper. If your cell phone dies, you might not have access to your contacts list.

conserveBattery.title: Conserve Phone Battery
conserveBattery.excerpt: Maximize your device's battery life during an outage.
content: Your mobile phone is your lifeline, so make it last. Activate 'Low Power Mode' immediately when power goes out. Dim your screen brightness to the lowest readable setting.
Close unused apps running in the background and turn off Wi-Fi and Bluetooth if they are searching for signals that aren't there. This drains battery significantly.
Use test messages instead of voice calls. Texts require far less data and battery power, and they are more likely to get through on congested networks.

backupPower.title: Use Backup Power Safely
backupPower.excerpt: Guidelines for generators and batteries to avoid hazards.
content: Portable generators are incredibly useful but can be deadly if misused. NEVER operate a generator inside your home, garage, or basement. They produce carbon monoxide (CO), an odorless, colorless gas that kills within minutes.
Place generators at least 20 feet away from your home, downwind from windows and doors. Always have working CO detectors in your home.

Do not plug a generator directly into your wall outlet (backfeeding). This poses serious electrocution risks to utility workers trying to restore power. Connect appliances directly to the generator using heavy-duty extension cords.

stayComfortable.title: Stay Warm (or Cool)
stayComfortable.excerpt: Tips for maintaining comfort in extreme temperatures.
content: In extreme cold, dress in layers. Wear a hat, as a lot of body heat is lost through the head. designate one room as your 'warm room', isolate it by closing doors to other rooms, and gather everyone there.
In extreme heat, stay on the lowest floor of your home where the air is cooler. Cover windows with drapes or aluminum foil to reflect sunlight. Drink plenty of water to prevent dehydration.
Check on neighbors, especially the elderly and those with health conditions, as they are more vulnerable to extreme temperatures.

medicalNeeds.title: Plan for Medical Needs
medicalNeeds.excerpt: Ensure critical medications and equipment remain operational.
content: If you or a family member relies on electric medical equipment, contact your utility provider. Many have 'priority reconnection' lists for life-sustaining equipment, though this is not a guarantee.
Have a backup plan for refrigerated medicines like insulin. a cooler with ice packs can keep meds safe for hours. Know where you can get more ice if the outage persists.
Talk to your doctor about an emergency plan. Ask if your medication can be stored at room temperature for a short period or if there are alternative treatments available during an emergency.

emergencyKit.title: Build Your 72-Hour Emergency Kit
emergencyKit.excerpt: A comprehensive guide to preparing your 72-hour survival kit, inspired by 72horas.pt.
content: In a major emergency, it may take up to 72 hours for help to arrive. Having a dedicated emergency kit is crucial for survival. The initiative 72horas.pt provides excellent guidance on what to include.

Your kit should contain water (1 gallon per person per day), non-perishable food, a first aid kit, flashlights, batteries, and a battery-powered radio. Don't forget personal hygiene items and essential medications.
Use a backpack that is easy to carry. Organize items by priority. For a complete checklist and interactive tools, visit [72horas.pt](https://72horas.pt/).

volunteering.title: Portugal Ajuda: Connect to Help
volunteering.excerpt: Find out how to volunteer or request assistance during emergencies with Portugal Ajuda.
content: During crisis situations like wildfires, effective coordination of help is vital. 'Portugal Ajuda' is a civil society initiative designed to connect those in need with those who can help.

The platform allows you to register as a volunteer to offer transport, accommodation, or supplies. Conversely, if you are affected by an emergency, you can request specific assistance directly through the site.
Community solidarity is our strongest asset. Visite [Portugal Ajuda](https://www.portugalajuda.pt/) to sign up, offer help, or find support in your area.

weatherImpacts.title: Ajuda Portugal: Register Weather Impacts
weatherImpacts.excerpt: A platform to register damages caused by extreme weather events.
content: Extreme weather events can have devastating effects on homes and businesses. 'Ajuda Portugal' provides a centralized form to register these impacts.
By documenting damages, you help authorities and support organizations understand the scale of the disaster and coordinate relief efforts more effectively.
If you have been affected by recent storms or other meteorological events, visit [Ajuda Portugal](https://ajudaportugal.com/) to report your situation.
